name: Tembleque and La Mancha Towns Loop
name_original: Cabañas de Yepe, Dosbarrios, La Guardia, El Romeral – circular desde Tembleque
description: This difficult 46.0-mile (74.1 km) touring cycling route from Tembleque offers challenging terrain and scenic views across the Ocaña Plateau
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Tembleque traverse a landscape characterized by agricultural plains and open countryside, typical of the Castilla-La Mancha region. The area features relatively flat terrain with gentle undulations, making it suitable for longer distance cycling. Routes often connect small towns and villages, passing through cultivated fields and occasional wetland areas. The region's low elevation gains contribute to accessible cycling experiences. Best touring cycling routes around Tembleque The most popular touring cycling route is La Guardia and its surroundings - circular route, a 18.0 miles (28.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 24 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the area around La Guardia. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is The lagoons and wetlands of Villacañas - circular passing through La Guardia, a difficult 41.7 miles (67.1 km) path. This route offers views of the region's lagoons and wetlands. Local touring cyclists also love the Villacañas to La Puebla de Almoradiel – circular via the Vía Verde del Trenillo, a 38.8 miles (62.4 km) trail leading through a former railway line, often completed in about 4 hours 51 minutes. Touring cycling around Tembleque is defined by agricultural plains, greenways, and wetland areas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging long-distance routes. The routes in Tembleque are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 39 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Tembleque's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Tembleque?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes available around Tembleque, offering a variety of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling near Tembleque?
A: The touring cycling routes around Tembleque primarily traverse agricultural plains and open countryside, characteristic of the Castilla-La Mancha region. The terrain is generally flat with gentle undulations, making it suitable for longer distances. You'll often pass through cultivated fields and occasional wetland areas, with low elevation gains contributing to an accessible cycling experience.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Tembleque?
A: Yes, Tembleque offers several easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022589446/la-guardia-and-its-surroundings-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Guardia and its surroundings - circular route&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option that explores the area around La Guardia, providing a good introduction to the region's cycling.

Q: Are there challenging or long-distance touring cycling routes in Tembleque?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022690728/the-lagoons-and-wetlands-of-villacanas-circular-passing-through-la-guardia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The lagoons and wetlands of Villacañas - circular passing through La Guardia&lt;/a&gt; offer a difficult 41.7-mile (67.1 km) path with views of the region's unique wetland areas. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022696479/cabanas-de-yepe-dosbarrios-la-guardia-el-romeral-circular-desde-tembleque" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabañas de Yepe, Dosbarrios, La Guardia, El Romeral – circular desde Tembleque&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 74 km.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes around Tembleque?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Tembleque are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022589446/la-guardia-and-its-surroundings-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Guardia and its surroundings - circular route&lt;/a&gt; and the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022690381/la-guardia-a-lillo-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Guardia a Lillo – circular route&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some interesting places to see or landmarks along the touring cycling routes?
A: While cycling around Tembleque, you can explore various points of interest. The historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5445505" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor of Tembleque&lt;/a&gt; is a notable architectural highlight. You might also encounter natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3322095" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Finisterre Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5440801" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Longar&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes connect small towns and villages, offering glimpses into local life and heritage.

Q: Are there any less crowded or 'hidden gem' routes for touring cyclists in Tembleque?
A: While specific 'hidden gems' are often discovered by local exploration, routes that venture further into the agricultural plains or connect smaller, less-frequented villages might offer a quieter experience. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022692576/villacanas-to-la-puebla-de-almoradiel-circular-via-the-via-verde-del-trenillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villacañas to La Puebla de Almoradiel – circular via the Vía Verde del Trenillo&lt;/a&gt;, utilizing a former railway line, could offer a distinct and potentially less crowded path.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Tembleque?
A: The touring cycling routes in Tembleque are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the accessible, relatively flat terrain, which is ideal for longer rides, and the opportunity to explore the agricultural landscapes and wetland areas of Castilla-La Mancha.

Q: Are there specific routes that pass through wetland areas or natural reserves?
A: Yes, the region is known for its wetland areas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022690728/the-lagoons-and-wetlands-of-villacanas-circular-passing-through-la-guardia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The lagoons and wetlands of Villacañas - circular passing through La Guardia&lt;/a&gt; route is specifically designed to showcase these natural features, offering unique views of the local lagoons and wetlands.

Q: Can I find routes that follow old railway lines or 'Vías Verdes'?
A: Yes, you can. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022692576/villacanas-to-la-puebla-de-almoradiel-circular-via-the-via-verde-del-trenillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villacañas to La Puebla de Almoradiel – circular via the Vía Verde del Trenillo&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent example, utilizing a former railway line that has been converted into a greenway, providing a smooth and scenic path for touring cyclists.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling options in Tembleque?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations are not provided, the generally flat terrain and moderate difficulty of many routes make them suitable for families with some cycling experience. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022589446/la-guardia-and-its-surroundings-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Guardia and its surroundings - circular route&lt;/a&gt; could be a good starting point for a family outing.
tips: Typically La Mancha building, with a square floor plan with a porch of granite columns and corridors on the upper floor, with supports and ornamentation made of wood following the orientations of popular constructions from the 17th century. Designed to fulfill a double function, the pure urban planning and center of life of the population and that of a bullring, so the corridors on its two upper levels are open. On one side is the Town Hall, built in 1654.

Cabañas de Yepes, in the Mesa de Ocaña (Toledo), is a small town with rural charm, historical heritage, and a natural setting ideal for relaxing.
Parish Church of Our Lady of the Assumption

Built in the 16th century, it features a central nave and chapel dedicated to Our Lady of Socorro.
Interesting fact: its clock (1829) uses the same clock system as the one at Puerta del Sol in Madrid.
Hermitage of San Sebastián
Dating from the 18th century, in the popular style, it is located on the road to Huerta de Valdecarábanos.

The La Guardia Viewpoint is located in La Guardia (Toledo), within the Ocaña Plateau, and is one of the most spectacular spots for contemplating the La Mancha landscape.
Located in the northern part of the municipality, on a plateau that slopes down toward the Cedrón stream valley.
From the viewpoint, you can see:
The La Guardia lagoon complex.
The Cedrón Valley and the La Mancha plain.
On clear days, even the Altomira Mountains.

The building was ordered to be built by D. Sebastián de Huerta, Secretary General of the Inquisition and a native of the Guard.
An outstanding element of the building is the 18th century Baroque façade that maintains some Mannerist aftertaste. It is made of stone and is preserved in good condition.

La Guardia (Toledo) is a very interesting destination in the Ocaña Plateau, offering historical heritage, nature, and cultural routes.
Church of Our Lady of the Assumption
Renaissance church with Baroque details, a main altarpiece, and a bell tower.
Casa de los Jaenes (Ethnological Museum)
An old 18th-century manor house with collections about traditional La Mancha life.

El Romeral (Toledo) is one of the most authentic villages on the Route of the Mills of La Mancha, with a group of mills that evoke Cervantes' image of Don Quixote.
There are four mills located on Cerro Santo, overlooking the La Mancha plain.
Their names are:

El Pechuga (the most emblematic, it retains its original machinery and is used for events).
El Crítica (renovated as an exhibition hall).
Los Gorrinos.
Muela.

faqs.question: How long does this touring bicycle route typically take?
faqs.answer: This challenging touring bicycle route is approximately 74.1 kilometers long and typically takes around 6 hours and 10 minutes to complete. Keep in mind that this is an expert-level route, and your actual duration may vary based on your fitness level and stops.

faqs.question: Is this touring bicycle route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as an expert-level bike ride and requires a high level of fitness. Some sections may even require you to dismount and push your bicycle due to challenging terrain. It is not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this circular touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one starting and ending in Tembleque. While specific parking details aren't provided, you would typically look for parking options within Tembleque to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: You can expect varied and potentially demanding terrain. While specific surface breakdowns are not detailed, the need to push your bike in some sections implies the presence of unpaved, possibly steep, or technical sections. The route traverses the Ocaña Plateau, featuring valleys and 'barrancos' (ravines).

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and natural features will I encounter on the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers scenic views of the Ocaña Plateau in Castilla-La Mancha, known for its agricultural landscapes. You'll see traditional crops like pistachio, almond, and olive groves, along with natural springs and geological features of valleys and ravines. The town of La Guardia is even referred to as the 'Balcony or Gate of La Mancha', suggesting panoramic views.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll pass several interesting spots. These include the historic Plaza Mayor of Tembleque, the unique Church of Our Lady of the Assumption in Cabañas de Yepes, and the Casa de los Jaenes. You'll also get Views of the La Guardia landscape and see traditional Windmills.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given the demanding nature of the route and the climate of Castilla-La Mancha, spring and autumn are generally the most favorable seasons for cycling. Summers can be very hot, and winters may bring colder temperatures, making these shoulder seasons ideal for a more comfortable experience.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other official trails. You'll find sections that overlap with the Camino del Presado, Camino Norte de la Laguna-La Guardia, Colada de la Mancha, and the Camino de Santiago del Sureste, among others.

faqs.question: Is this touring bicycle route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this particular route are not detailed, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in agricultural areas or near towns, to ensure their safety and respect local practices. Given the route's difficulty, consider if it's suitable for your dog's fitness level.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there is no mention of any specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It appears to be freely accessible.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or services for cyclists along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through several towns including Tembleque, Cabañas de Yepes, Dosbarrios, La Guardia, and El Romeral. These towns are likely to offer opportunities for refreshments, cafes, and other basic services for cyclists, though specific establishments are not detailed.
